scenario: Helios Insurance ships a new DMN decision table to Camunda 8.8 that classifies 12000 claims per day into risk bands. The modeler did not select a hit policy in the properties panel, and the team wants to confirm what the deployed table will do when more than one rule matches the input data.
question: Which hit policy will the deployed decision table use, and what happens if two rules match the same input?

options.text: UNIQUE is the default hit policy, and a matching pair of rules raises an evaluation error reported as an incident on the business rule task
COLLECT is the default hit policy, and overlapping rule outputs are returned to the process as a list with arbitrary ordering
FIRST is the default hit policy, and the engine quietly returns only the output of the first overlapping rule defined in the table
RULE ORDER is the default hit policy, and overlapping rules emit an ordered list of outputs to the calling business rule task

a.text: Correct. The DMN docs state that when no hit policy is set the default UNIQUE applies; a second matching rule violates UNIQUE and the evaluation fails.

b.text: Incorrect. COLLECT must be set explicitly on the decisionTable XML element and is never the implicit default in Camunda DMN.

c.text: Incorrect. FIRST is a separate explicit policy and is not chosen by Camunda when the hitPolicy attribute is omitted from the decision table.

d.text: Incorrect. RULE ORDER must be set explicitly and is not the default behaviour for a decision table without a hit policy attribute.

explanation: The DMN spec implemented by Camunda 8.8 defaults to UNIQUE when no hit policy is set; multiple matches violate UNIQUE and the engine raises an evaluation incident on the business rule task.
